characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Charlie Brown Baldness: He has a few strands of hair on his head, but that's it. Signing-Off Catchphrase: He ends every episode with "See you next time, love Todd!" Trademark Favorite Food: He's very fond of his macaroni and cheese. He likes to eat it in the bathtub if he can, but he's fine eating it anywhere. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: In one episode, Todd is shown to have a fear of the dark, but he gets over it at the end of the episode. It is also never mentioned that Todd is afraid of the dark in any other episode either.

characters.name: Todd
characters.description: Charlie Brown Baldness: He has a few strands of hair on his head, but that's it. Signing-Off Catchphrase: He ends every episode with "See you next time, love Todd!" Trademark Favorite Food: He's very fond of his macaroni and cheese. He likes to eat it in the bathtub if he can, but he's fine eating it anywhere. Why Did It Have to Be Snakes?: In one episode, Todd is shown to have a fear of the dark, but he gets over it at the end of the episode. It is also never mentioned that Todd is afraid of the dark in any other episode either.

characters.name: Benny
characters.description: Covered in Mud: Zigzags whenever Benny comes into contact with mud. Female Feline, Male Mutt: The male mutt to Mitzi's female feline. Funny Animal: He is one heck of a very playful and energetic pup. Sleepyhead: Benny is often found sleeping. In one episode when the group had a Slumber Party, Benny fell asleep and woke up just as they announced it was time for bed, then he got ready to go back to sleep. Talking Animal: He's a talking dog.

characters.name: Pickle
characters.description: Aerith and Bob: While Todd and his friends (his dog included) all have real human names, Pickle is the only human character with a food as a name. Edible Theme Naming: Pickle is the only one in the main cast to have this. It apparently runs in the family, all of whom are green like him. He has an "Uncle Gherkin", and his younger cousin named "Briney". Height Angst: Only in one episode after he struggled to play Limbo like everyone else and kept losing. But as he made his way home Pickle learned that his height allowed him to help his friends. Unwanted Glasses Plot: Pickle was acting strange and had trouble seeing, leaving his friends worried about him until they caught him unprepared to see he needs glasses to read. Pickle was worried he looked weird with them on and didn't want people to laugh at him.

characters.name: Sophie
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: She is generally nice, but once her patience is tested, she can become snarky. Expressive Hair: She has thick, large pigtails that wiggle and stick up when she gets excited, and lower when she is unhappy. Fiery Redhead: She has red hair put up in pigtails, but while she doesn't have much of a temper, she can become quite stubborn whenever her plans don't work. Flight: With her thick pigtails, she is able to fly. A few characters remark on it, but others are used to it. She says she was born with them, and her mother has the same hair, as does her baby sister Lily. Gadgeteer Genius: She enjoys inventing things and searching for new ideas.

characters.name: Stella
characters.description: Bows for Cuteness: She wears a bow on her head, and she loves to bake bow-shaped cookies. Feminine Women Can Cook: Stella has an interest in baking and often makes cookies for her friends. Bow-shaped, of course! Girly Girl: Is big in to fashion and accessorizing. In one installment, creates a play called "A Salute to Sparkliness". Girly Girl with a Tomboy Streak: While she is known for her interest in fashion, she also enjoys rough and tumble sports, playing in the dirt, and building karts for racing. Meaningful Name: Her name is Latin for "Star", and her dress has stars all over it.

characters.name: Mitzi
characters.description: Female Feline, Male Mutt: The female feline to Benny's male mutt. Parental Substitute: For Inky, Oswald, and Jake, three puppies she takes in after they found them outside of Sophie's apartment. She treats them like they are her own children and took offense when another dog refused to let the puppies play with her son because they act like cats. Put on a Bus: Later in the series, this played a role in an episode. Mitzi and the puppies were given to Sophie's uncle, who owns a farm and would have the proper space for them to run around and not worry about making messes due to their growing size. Talking Animal: She is a talking cat.
